full_prompt:
Design the main carbon-fiber-reinforced polymer (CFRP) fuselage truss frame of a 25 kg-class light unmanned aircraft, governed by EASA Special Condition Light-UAS 01 (2020). The SC covers UAS in the Specific / Certified categories depending on MTOM; this brief targets the tier 25 kg < MTOM <= 150 kg for a fixed-wing UAS. Applicable clauses: SC Light-UAS.2255 (maneuver envelope with n_pos = +3.8 and n_neg = -1.52 by default VLA-equivalent), SC Light-UAS.2270 (ultimate = 1.5 x limit), and SC Light-UAS.2310 (crashworthiness: the airframe must dissipate the crash kinetic energy such that peak occupant-equivalent acceleration, here the payload bay, is bounded - for UAS the occupant proxy is the payload; energy dissipation must deform the airframe progressively rather than fracturing brittlely).

Geometry. Main fuselage truss frame: 6 longerons (carbon-epoxy pultruded tubes, 20 mm OD, 2 mm wall), 8 transverse bulkheads (CFRP plates 150 mm x 120 mm x 3 mm), and diagonal shear braces. Frame envelope 2000 mm long x 300 mm wide x 250 mm tall. Payload bay is a 600 mm x 250 mm x 200 mm volume at the CG. Wing attach points (4 bolts) at the top, engine mount (4 bolts) at the nose. All joints adhesively bonded with structural epoxy; mechanical fasteners used only at wing and engine mounts.

Material. Carbon-epoxy pultruded tube (T700/epoxy): E_L = 120 GPa, E_T = 9 GPa, rho = 1550 kg/m^3, tensile strength Xt = 1800 MPa, compression Xc = 1100 MPa. Bulkhead CFRP plate quasi-isotropic layup [0/+45/-45/90]_s: E_in_plane = 52 GPa, tensile 650 MPa, compression 480 MPa, shear 110 MPa.

Service envelope. MTOM 25 kg, cruise VC = 40 m/s, VD = 55 m/s, stall VS = 18 m/s. n_pos limit = +3.8, n_neg limit = -1.52, ultimate = 1.5 x limit => n_pos_ult = +5.7, n_neg_ult = -2.28. Crashworthiness: impact energy 0.5 * m * V_impact^2 with V_impact = 15 m/s (VS-based minimum per SC Light-UAS.2310 crash scenario) = 0.5 * 25 * 225 = 2812 J must be dissipated by airframe deformation over a stroke that limits peak deceleration at the payload bay to <= 20 g (per SC Light-UAS.2310 payload-protection proxy value; the actual SC references an acceleration limit for occupants that does not apply here - this 20 g is a representative project proxy). Landing: tricycle gear with 3 g ultimate landing inertia on the payload bay.

Load cases. LC1 (pos maneuver ultimate, +5.7 g): inertial load on airframe + payload (10 kg lumped) = 5.7 * 35 kg * 9.81 = 1957 N applied at payload bay CG. LC2 (neg maneuver ultimate, -2.28 g): -783 N. LC3 (landing ultimate 3 g): 1030 N vertical on gear attach. LC4 (wing bending moment at root): 3.8 * 25 * 9.81 * 0.4 * 1.5 = 560 N vertical at wing attach (ultimate). LC5 (crashworthiness energy absorption): dynamic 15 m/s impact; evaluate via explicit crash FEM or by ensuring progressive-failure energy dissipation exceeds 2812 J over <= 300 mm crush stroke.

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) LC1 and LC2 max composite fiber stress (longeron) must not exceed Xt = 1800 MPa (tension) and Xc = 1100 MPa (compression) with an additional composite ultimate knockdown of 1.25 (environment + scatter for primary composite) => effective allowables Xt_eff = 1440 MPa, Xc_eff = 880 MPa. (R2) Bulkhead max in-plane stress must not exceed 650 / 1.25 = 520 MPa tension, 480 / 1.25 = 384 MPa compression. (R3) LC3 landing max stress <= allowables of R1/R2. (R4) LC4 wing-attach bolt interface stress in longeron <= 1440 MPa. (R5) Crashworthiness LC5: total energy absorbed >= 2812 J in <= 300 mm stroke (equivalent to average deceleration <= 20 g at payload bay under uniform deceleration: a_avg = V^2 / (2 * stroke) = 225 / 0.6 = 375 m/s^2 = 38 g, which exceeds the 20 g proxy; designer must therefore extend the crush stroke to >= 573 mm or add controlled-failure stages). (R6) Frame mass <= 2.8 kg. (R7) No mechanical joint may fail before progressive composite failure at the longerons under LC5 (toughening-priority ordering).

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEM for LC1-LC4 in a composite-shell or continuum-shell framework. Explicit dynamic crash FEM (LS-DYNA or Abaqus Explicit) required for LC5; alternatively a closed-form energy-absorption hand-calc accepted for R5 if the stroke/acceleration relationship is transparent. Modal, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required for this brief.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of frame, mass in kg, max composite fiber stress per LC, energy-absorption curve or value for LC5, crush stroke in mm, and peak payload-bay deceleration in g.
short_prompt:
Design the main CFRP fuselage truss frame of a 25 kg-class light fixed-wing UAS (payload bay at CG, wing attach at the top, engine mount at the nose). Design to EASA Special Condition Light-UAS 01 (2020): SC Light-UAS.2255 maneuver envelope (n +3.8 / -1.52), SC Light-UAS.2270 ultimate = 1.5 x limit, and SC Light-UAS.2310 crashworthiness. A composite environment/scatter knockdown of 1.25 applies on top of the 1.5 ultimate.

Envelope: truss frame 2000 mm long x 300 mm wide x 250 mm tall; 6 T700/epoxy pultruded longerons (20 mm OD, 2 mm wall); 8 CFRP quasi-isotropic bulkheads 150 x 120 x 3 mm; 600 x 250 x 200 mm payload bay at CG; bonded joints, mechanical fasteners only at wing (4) and engine mount (4).

Load cases. Inertial loads applied at payload bay CG (airframe + 10 kg lumped payload = 35 kg).
- LC1 positive maneuver ultimate (+5.7 g): 1,957 N.
- LC2 negative maneuver ultimate (-2.28 g): -783 N.
- LC3 landing 3 g ultimate: 1,030 N on gear attach.
- LC4 wing-attach ultimate moment: 560 N at the wing attach.
- LC5 crashworthiness (SC.2310): 15 m/s impact of 25 kg => 2,812 J to be absorbed with peak payload-bay deceleration bounded (20 g target proxy).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: SC Light-UAS.2310 crashworthiness is a kinetic-energy-absorption problem.

prompt.title: 25 kg light UAS CFRP fuselage frame per EASA SC Light-UAS
prompt.brief: Pultruded CFRP truss + bulkheads, n +3.8/-1.52 maneuver, ultimate 1.5x, 15 m/s crash energy absorbed in <=300 mm or extended stroke.

boundary_conditions.nose: engine mount fixed in static; free-free in crash explicit

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 0.5 * m * V^2 with m=25 kg, V=15 m/s; peak payload-bay accel <= 20 g proxy
Progressive composite failure before brittle mechanical-joint failure

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: composite shell + continuum shell for crash

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Lift already reduced to inertia load.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Longeron local buckling production concern; not in this brief.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: BVID / damage tolerance per composite handbook

eval_coverage: crashworthiness energy absorption via explicit FEM
